title: 3 heap allocs per test for tag extraction
description: eval_header_test, eval_address_test, and eval_envelope_test each chain three extract_*() calls (extract_match_type -> extract_comparator -> extract_address_part), each of which allocates a Vec<&Form> (evaluator.rs:286-366). This is 3 intermediate heap allocations per test invocation to filter a slice that is at most 6-8 elements in practice. Under heavy evaluation load (many messages, scripts with many header tests), this generates significant GC pressure. The extraction should either use a single pass that extracts all three simultaneously, or extract by scanning (returning values+index-range) without allocating intermediate collections.
notes: 3 small Vec allocs (3-8 elements each) per test invocation. At typical Sieve load (100 msg/s × 3 tests) this is ~900 tiny allocs/s totaling ~100 KB/s of short-lived allocations — below measurement threshold. Refactor to a single-pass struct extractor is a valid future improvement but not worth the churn now.
